english: ṅó-tsa-śes-pa (ngo tsha shes pa) shamefaced, chaste, ashamed; ṅó-tsa-med-pa (ngo tsha med pa), mi-śes-pa (mi shes pa) shameless, barefaced, impudent; *ṅa ṅó-tsa rag* (*nga ngo tsha rag*) I am ashamed, *ko ṅó-tsa dug, ṅó-tsa-càn dug* (*kho ngo tsha dug, ngo tsha can dug*) W. he is ashamed; ṅó-tsa byéd-pa (ngo tsha byed pa) to be ashamed. 2. a shameful thing, kyoḍ ṅó-mi-tsa-la ṅó-tsar byed (khyod ngo mi tsha la ngo tshar byed) you are ashamed where there is no occasion for it Mil.; ṅó-tsa-ba (ngo tsha ba) to be indecent, indecorous, unbecoming, yul-du lóg-na ṅó-tsa-la (yul du log na ngo tsha la) as it would be a shame if we returned Glr.; gcér-bur _gró-ba ṅó-tsa ziṅ (gcer bur 'gro ba ngo tsha zhing) as it would be indecorous to go naked Pth. — ṅo-mtsár-ba (ngo mtshar ba) v. mtsár-ba (mtshar ba). — ṅó-ru (ngo ru), ṅor (ngor) 1. into the face Sch., e.g. skud-pa (skud pa) to smear; rtsub-pa (rtsub pa) to say rude things to another's face Thgy.; ṅo-raṅ-du (ngo rang du) id. 2. in the face of, before the eyes, gzáṅ-gyi (gzhan gyi) of others. 3. by reason of, in consequence of, des bsḳúl-bai ṅor (des bskul ba'i ngor) in consequence of a summons, of a request of him Glr. and elsewh. — ṅo-śés (ngo shes) an acquaintance, a friend (the usual word in W.). — ṅo-só (ngo so) joy, ṅo-só čé-bar _oṅ (ngo so che bar 'ong) you will have great joy, you will be delighted, highly satisfied; sbyiṅ-pa ṅo-só byéd-pa (sbyin pa ngo so byed pa) to make presents to another to his full satisfaction Mil., also Tar. 211, 2. — ṅo-srúṅs (ngo srungs) regard to the opinion of others, an aiming at applause Mil.
1. mountain-side, slope (cf. ṅos (ngos)); river-side, bank, shore, rgyá-mtsoi (rgya mtsho'i) Dzl. - 2 ford, ču-ṅógs (chu ngogs) id. C.

pf. ṅoms (ngoms), 1. to satisfy one's desire by drinking, ḳrág-gis (khrag gis), also ḳrág-las (khrag las) Dzl.; ma ṅoms (ma ngoms) I am still thirsty; ṅóm-par (ngom par), also ṅoms-tsád (ngoms tshad), _túṅ-ba ('thung ba) to drink one's fill; also of sleeping, gñid ma ṅom (gnyid ma ngom) I have not yet had my full share of sleep; fig.: čós-kyi bdud-rtsis (chos kyi bdud rtsis), to fill one's self with the nectar of doctrine Dzl.; bltá-bas mi ṅoms mdzés-pa (blta bas mi ngoms mdzes pa) so beautiful, that one cannot gaze at it long enough, frq.; also bltá-bas mi ṅoms bźín-du (blta bas mi ngoms bzhin du) not being able to look at it sufficiently Pth.; ṅoms(-pa)-méd(-pa) (ngoms(-pa) med(-pa)) insatiable. - 2. to show with design (boasting, or indecently, e.g. one's nakedness) Glr., Pth. - 3. col. for snóm-pa (snom pa) to snuffle, to pry into, to spy.
1. v. under ṅo (ngo), Comp. - 2. n. of a monastery of the Saskya, Wdk. chro-nological table in Cs.'s Gram.

1. side, mdún-ṅos (mdun ngos) front-side, front of the body Lt.; of a pyramid, a mountain, lhó-ṅos (lho ngos) southern side or slope of a mountain, side, margin, edge, of a pond etc.; rgyáb ṅos gyás-na (rgyab ngos g.yas na) on the right hand behind, gyás ṅos mdún-na (g.yas ngos mdun na) on the right hand before Glr.; surface, plain, of the table; sai ṅos (sa'i ngos) surface of the earth Cs.; hence ṅós-su (ngos su) (opp. to lkog-tu (lkog tu)) Mil., *ṅó-la* (*ngo la*) (opp. to sbás-te (sbas te) (*bé-te* (*be te*)) W. manifestly, notoriously, publicly, openly (cf. ṅo (ngo)); side, direction, like pyogs (phyogs), W. - 2. a thing itself (cf. ṅo (ngo) 3), examples v. under _jál-ba ('jal ba). - 3. pers. pron. first person I, we; esp. in Ld. in epistolary correspondence, eleg. - 4. instrum. of ṅo (ngo), = ṅó-yis (ngo yis); ṅos dzin-pa (ngos 'dzin pa) Mil. (dṅos dzin-pa (dngos 'dzin pa) Thgy.) vb. 1. to be selfish, self-interested, also adj. selfish, cf. ṅos (ngos) 3. 2. more frq. to perceive, to know, to discern, also ṅó-yis dzin-pa (ngo yis 'dzin pa); ṅos zin-par gyis śig (ngos zin par gyis shig), know it! be sensible of it! Thgr.; with the termin.: to acknowledge as, to take for, to look upon as Tar. 189, 1. In a special sense: diagnosis, discriminating a disease Med. *ṅóṅ-ṅo láṅ-wa* (*ngong ngo lang wa*) C. (lit. gnoṅ ṅos blaṅ-ba (gnong ngos blang ba)) = *ṅo lén-če* (*ngo len che*) v. *ṅo* (*ngo*) 1.

pf. dṅaṅs (dngangs) 1. to be out of breath, to pant, to feel oppressed e.g. when plunging into cold water C., but esp. when frightened and terrified, hence 2 to be frightened, to fear, to be afraid, sbrul-gyis (sbrul gyis) of a snake; čes dṅáṅs-nas (ces dngangs nas) thus he spoke in dismay Dzl.; dṅáṅ-par gyúr-ro (dngangs par gyur ro) you will (or would) be terrified Dzl.; dṅaṅ-skrág (dngangs skrag), skrag-dṅáṅ (skrag dngangs) great fear, fright, terror: dṅaṅ-skrág-pa (dngangs skrag pa) intensive form of dṅáṅ-ba (dngangs pa), frq.
Lex. not to return things taken away from another.
1. for mṅar (mngar), sweet Mil. and elsewh. - 2. also zil-dṅár (zil dngar) Lex. w.e.;
